start.speaker: Father
start.text: Congratulations! You've reached a new stage in life. Whether you're a student with pocket money or a working professional, learning to manage your ROI (Return on Investment) starts now.

choices.mentorText: Good choice. Starting small with pocket money is the best way to build saving habits.
Jumping into the deep end! Salary management is where the real 50-30-20 rule comes into play.

lvl1_scene1.speaker: Father
lvl1_scene1.text: Yay! You got ₹5000 as your pocket money this month. But remember—you shouldn't spend it all. You should save a part of it in an RD, a recurring deposit. It will help you in the future.

lvl1_rd_explain.speaker: Father
lvl1_rd_explain.text: An RD, or Recurring Deposit, is a way of saving money regularly. You deposit a fixed amount every month for a certain period. The bank gives you interest, so your money grows over time! It builds a habit of disciplined saving.

choices.text: I'll save a little every month!

lvl1_choice_month.speaker: System
lvl1_choice_month.text: You have ₹5000 this month. What would you like to do? Choose how much you want to allocate.

choices.mentorText: Smart! Saving 40% of your pocket money is an excellent start.
It's a start, but you might find it hard to afford big trips later.
Careful... You have no safety net now.

lvl1_scene2.speaker: Friend (Rahul)
lvl1_scene2.text: Hey! We're going for a party this weekend, are you coming? It sounds fun... but should you spend your money on this?

choices.mentorText: Enjoy the party, but watch your remaining balance.
Disciplined! You're keeping your goals in sight.

lvl1_scene3.speaker: Friend (Priya)
lvl1_scene3.text: By the way, we're planning a trip after a few months! You should start saving from now so you can join us. Do you want to start saving for the trip?

choices.mentorText: Planning ahead is key to enjoying life without debt.

choices.text: No, I can't afford it yet.
choices.mentorText: It's okay to say no when the budget is tight.

lvl1_scene4.speaker: Teacher
lvl1_scene4.text: You have been assigned a project. Make sure you complete it on time. You'll need to buy materials for this project. This is a NEED. Money will be spent from your budget.

choices.mentorText: Always prioritize 'Needs' like education and essentials.

lvl1_summary.speaker: System
lvl1_summary.text: 6 months have passed. Time period completed. Here is your summary. Let's see if you saved enough for the trip!

lvl1_trip_check.speaker: Friends
lvl1_trip_check.text: We're all going on the trip now! Are you coming? (Checking your total balance...)

lvl2_scene1.speaker: Father
lvl2_scene1.text: You just received your first salary — ₹50,000! Congratulations. Now it's time to learn how to manage it using the 50-30-20 rule.

lvl2_rule_explain.speaker: Father
lvl2_rule_explain.text: It means dividing your income into three parts: 50% for Needs (rent, food), 30% for Wants (fun, shopping), and 20% for Savings. For ₹50,000, that's ₹25k on needs, ₹15k on wants, and ₹10k for savings.

choices.text: That sounds like a good plan!

lvl2_emi_trap.speaker: Friend (Rahul)
lvl2_emi_trap.text: A new phone just launched! You can buy it on ₹5,000 EMI every month. That seems affordable, right?

choices.mentorText: Watch out! Multiple EMIs can become a trap and reduce your monthly cash flow.
Smart. Avoiding debts is the first rule of wealth building.

lvl2_needs_wants.speaker: System
lvl2_needs_wants.text: I need to manage my expenses wisely. Classify the following: Rent, Groceries (NEEDS) vs Gaming console, Movie night (WANTS). Needs should always come first.

choices.mentorText: Excellent. Covering essentials first ensures peace of mind.

lvl2_emergencies.speaker: Father
lvl2_emergencies.text: You should also build an emergency fund. Unexpected expenses can happen anytime. Savings help you handle them without stress.

choices.mentorText: This is your financial shield. Never skip building this fund.

final_summary.speaker: System
final_summary.text: Lesson Learned: Follow the 50-30-20 rule, avoid the EMI trap, understand needs vs wants, and save regularly. You have mastered the basics of ROI!
